channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
item.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
item.content:encoded:
Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.

(Image credit: Allison Lee Isley)
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey

item.title: July 4th fireworks in Washington: What's different for America's 250th?
item.description: This year's fireworks display in Washington, D.C., is scheduled toWhat to know about the massive July 4 firework show for America's 250th
item.content:encoded:
This year's fireworks display in Washington, D.C., is scheduled toWhat to know about the massive July 4 firework show for America's 250th

(Image credit: Mohammad Reza Mousavi/Middle East Images)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell

item.title: Food defined social hierarchy in 1776. Here's what was on the table
item.description: Around the time the United States was founded, Americans' diets included Parmesan ice cream and terrapin. But what you ate depended on your social status.
item.content:encoded:
Around the time the United States was founded, Americans' diets included Parmesan ice cream and terrapin. But what you ate depended on your social status.

(Image credit: Anna Rose Layden for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: Will the new student loan limits actually drive down tuition? Economists weigh in
item.description: The idea that there's a connection between federal student loans and what colleges charge dates back almost four decades. But it's unclear that link can lead to lower costs.
item.content:encoded:
The idea that there's a connection between federal student loans and what colleges charge dates back almost four decades. But it's unclear that link can lead to lower costs.

(Image credit: Eduardo Munoz Alvarez)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: 'If you are alive, make any noise': Venezuela searches rubble on day 4
item.description: Rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.
item.content:encoded:
Rescue teams are racing against time after twin earthquakes killed more than 1,400 people and left tens of thousands unaccounted for.

(Image credit: Miguel Medina)
item.dc:creator: Fernando Narro Roman

item.title: Onto the knockout round: 4 takeaways from the FIFA World Cup so far
item.description: The anxieties before the World Cup were many. But with the knockout round set to begin Sunday, it's fair to say: The North American World Cup has been a thrill.
item.content:encoded:
The anxieties before the World Cup were many. But with the knockout round set to begin Sunday, it's fair to say: The North American World Cup has been a thrill.

(Image credit: Patrick T. Fallon)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Trump nominates former Oklahoma state trooper to head ICE
item.description: President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to direct Immigration and Customs Enforcement. The agency hasn't had a Senate-confirmed director since the Obama administration.

(Image credit: Erin Hooley)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Critical fire weather complicates firefighting efforts in massive Utah wildfire
item.description: Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.
item.content:encoded:
Firefighters working on the nation's largest current wildfire, burning in southern Utah, are being challenged by historic weather conditions. Extreme wildfire behavior is expected to continue through the weekend.

(Image credit: Ty ONeil)
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott

item.title: Uzbekistan makes its World Cup debut, a first for Central Asia
item.description: The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."
item.content:encoded:
The country is the first Central Asian nation to qualify for the World Cup, and Uzbek fans have reveled in showcasing their country and culture. The country's president calls the team a symbol of the "new Uzbekistan."

(Image credit: Ashley Landis)
item.dc:creator: Charles Maynes

item.title: Opinion: Ranch dressing is a winner at the World Cup games
item.description: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the popularity of ranch dressing among international visitors to the U.S. during the World Cup games.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Simon reflects on the popularity of ranch dressing among international visitors to the U.S. during the World Cup games.
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon
